title: Gemma 4 (E2B / E4B, INT4 OpenVINO IR) as an out-of-family grading instrument
question: Can any Gemma 4 variant run locally on this machine at all, on any device, and what does it cost in RAM? Instrument framing (independent grader), NOT a production-model swap.
author: Claude Opus 4.8 research subagent, dev-side probe
disposition: Dev-side probe only. No runtime config, .toml, manifest, or services/ file was read for write or modified. Probe ran in an isolated venv; the runtime .venv was never pip-installed into.

hardware.memory: 32 GB LPDDR5X, 31.323 GB effective ceiling (shared across CPU/GPU/NPU)

software.venv: isolated probe venv under the session scratchpad; NOT the BlarAI runtime .venv
software.pipeline_class: openvino_genai.VLMPipeline (Gemma 4 is registered as a VLM architecture: Gemma4ForConditionalGeneration / Gemma4UnifiedForConditionalGeneration)
software.note: openvino_genai.VLMPipeline loads the pre-converted IR directly. The optimum-intel / transformers==5.5.0 / custom-branch requirement stated on the HF model card applies to the OVModelForVisualCausalLM export path, NOT to running an already-converted IR. Nothing from transformers or optimum was installed.

models.precision: INT4 asymmetric, group size 128, ratio 1.0 (NNCF), per HF model card
models.hf_card_status: EXPERIMENTAL MODEL - not fully validated with OpenVINO, exported from a custom optimum-intel branch

models.precision: INT4 asymmetric, group size 128 (NNCF), per HF model card
models.hf_card_status: EXPERIMENTAL MODEL - not fully validated with OpenVINO, exported from a custom optimum-intel branch

methodology.memory_accounting: In-Use = TotalPhys - AvailPhys via GlobalMemoryStatusEx, host-wide, sampled on a 100 ms background thread for peaks. Process working set / private commit reported alongside via GetProcessMemoryInfo. Working-set numbers are secondary; host In-Use is the doctrine measure (ADR-005).
methodology.memory_caveat: OpenVINO memory-maps weight .bin files. On the CPU device those pages land in the Windows standby list, which Windows counts inside 'Available' - so the CPU-device In-Use delta UNDERSTATES true footprint. The GPU-device numbers do not have this problem (weights are committed into the shared GPU allocation) and are the ones quoted below.
methodology.generation_config: do_sample=False (greedy, temperature-0 equivalent, per project determinism mandate), max_new_tokens as noted per run. Text-only input; no image supplied.
methodology.prompt: Short grader prompt (85 input tokens) asking for a one-line JSON verdict on a candidate answer.
methodology.run_counts: Throughput/latency: single run per (model, device) cell. Determinism: 3 consecutive generations from one loaded pipeline. Juror battery: 6 cells (2 subjects x 3 lenses), each generated twice.
methodology.juror_battery: Prompt shape copied verbatim from tools/dispatch_harness/green_quality/jury.py Juror.build_prompt - the real BlarAI green-quality rubric, not a toy - over one clean subject and one deliberately defective subject.
methodology.state: Machine otherwise idle; Qwen3-14B NOT resident during any run; BlarAI app and launcher were not started.

E2B_int4.output_text: {"verdict": "pass", "reason": "Correctly identifies the capital and its significance"}

E4B_int4.output_text: {"verdict": "pass", "reason": "Correctly identifies the capital and provides a relevant reason for its significance."}

E2B_int4.DEFECT: Output is corrupt and NON-DETERMINISTIC under greedy decoding. Across 3 identical runs (do_sample=False) the model produced 2 distinct outputs; 2 of 3 runs emitted only 2 characters ('{"') while consuming the full 120-token budget without stopping, i.e. the remaining tokens decoded to nothing. A third run produced correct JSON. Separate earlier runs produced a third variant (21 chars). Tokenizer round-trip was verified clean on the same pipeline, so the detokenizer is not the fault - the model itself emits garbage token IDs on the CPU plugin.

cpu.E4B_int4: NOT RUN - the CPU plugin defect was already established on E2B

E2B_int4.error: RuntimeError: Exception from src/inference/src/cpp/core.cpp:120 -> plugin.cpp:54 -> intel_npu/src/plugin/src/plugin.cpp:673 -> compiler_adapter/src/compiler_impl.cpp:332: Compilation failed. vclAllocatedExecutableCreate3 result: 0x78000004 - [NPU_VCL] Compiler returned msg: Compilation failed

E4B_int4.quality_note: Grades were substantively correct: the clean CLI scored runnable_surface=yes / naming=clear; the defective module scored runnable_surface=no or only-by-writing-code, bad_input_handling=unchecked, naming_structure=mixed. Lens sensitivity was visible (the legibility lens returned different values than the correctness lens on the same subject), which is desirable for a diverse panel. It did NOT flag the unguarded division in the defective subject (correctness_probe=none in all cells) - a real miss.

E2B_int4.quality_note: Format compliance equal to E4B, but judgment noticeably noisier: it scored the CLEAN subject correctness_probe=wrong and bad_input_handling=unchecked, and under one lens called a script with an argparse main() runnable_surface=no. Usable but lower-trust.

what_was_NOT_measured: Co-residency with Qwen3-14B. The 14B was never loaded during these runs; every number above is against an otherwise-idle box. The load-on-demand-then-evict pattern is INFERRED to fit from the transient peak, not demonstrated with the 14B present.
Long-context behaviour. All runs used an 85-token prompt. No context-scaling curve, no KV-cache growth measurement.
Image input. Gemma 4 is a VLM and the vision-embeddings sub-model was loaded, but no image was ever supplied. Vision is untested.
Accuracy against BlarAI's answer_quality golden set, or any swapped-judge re-scoring. The juror battery used two hand-written subjects, not the real corpus.
Sustained/thermal throughput. Single short generations only; no multi-minute load.
INT8 and FP16 variants (both exist on HF for E2B and E4B) - only INT4 was pulled.
The 12B variant - no pre-converted OpenVINO IR exists (verified against the OpenVINO HF org listing), so it was out of reach of a download-and-run probe.
The 26B-A4B (14.3 GB) and 31B (17.4 GB) IRs - both exist but exceed the probe's download budget and would exceed the RAM envelope for a load-on-demand instrument.
Speculative decoding - deliberately not attempted; irrelevant under the instrument framing.
artifacts_executed_against: OpenVINO/gemma-4-E2B-it-int4-ov (downloaded, 4.062 GB, run on CPU + GPU + NPU-attempt)
tools/dispatch_harness/green_quality/jury.py (read on disk; rubric prompt reproduced verbatim in the battery)
